content.verdict: L'Ea Bianca is the boutique adults-only secret of Baja Sardinia — just 38 rooms built into a hillside above Cala dei Ginepri cove, with an infinity pool that hovers over the sea and a restaurant (Lucrezia) that has become a destination in its own right for honeymoon anniversary dinners. It is a 10-minute drive to Porto Cervo but feels a world away: quiet, whitewashed, Greek-island intimate, the kind of place where the same staff remember your name after two days. For couples who want Costa Smeralda scenery with Cycladic calm and without 200-room scale, this is the most honeymoon-specific hotel in Sardinia.
content.best_room: A Junior Suite Sea View with private terrace is the quintessential room here — compact but beautifully done, with direct views over Cala dei Ginepri. The Ea Bianca Suite with plunge pool is the honeymoon splurge; avoid garden-view categories if you can, as the sea views are what you came for.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and Lucrezia welcome dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Olbia in about 45 minutes. Check in, welcome drink on your terrace, and a first dinner at Lucrezia — book the earliest reservation so you catch the sunset over the pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Infinity pool and spa day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow breakfast on the terrace, the whole morning at the infinity pool with attendants, long lunch at the pool bar, afternoon couple's treatment at the small boutique spa, sunset aperitivo at the pool bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private boat to La Maddalena
itinerary_7_nights.description: The concierge arranges a skippered boat from Baja Sardinia harbor. Full day touring Spargi, Budelli, Santa Maria — anchor at Cala Corsara for the most photogenic swim of your trip. Quiet in-suite dinner tonight.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spiaggia del Principe and Porto Cervo
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at Spiaggia del Principe (15 min drive) — one of the five best beaches in Italy. Lunch at Il Pescatore in Porto Cervo marina, afternoon boutique browse, return for pool sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Phi Beach sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Lazy pool morning, long poolside lunch, late-afternoon short drive to Phi Beach (just up the coast) for the famous rock-amphitheater sunset cocktail ritual.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Inland Gallura day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 40 minutes inland for a Vermentino tasting at Siddùra or Capichera winery, lunch at a cork-forest agriturismo, return for a last spa treatment and a farewell dinner at Lucrezia.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late breakfast on the terrace, one last swim in the cove below, and the car to Olbia. Many couples extend with two nights in Alghero or inland for contrast.

caveats: Adults-only (typically 14+) — confirm current minimum age with the hotel before booking, especially for shoulder months.
The private beach is a small cove reached by a path and stairs; bigger sand beaches are a short drive away.
Open seasonally, roughly late April through mid-October. No winter operation.

faqs.question: How adults-only is it really?
faqs.answer: Very. It is designed for couples and the minimum age policy is enforced. The atmosphere is hushed and intimate, more boutique than resort.

faqs.question: How does it compare to the Luxury Collection hotels?
faqs.answer: L'Ea Bianca is much smaller (38 rooms vs 54-121), more design-boutique than grand-traditional, and adults-only. Food at Lucrezia rivals anything on the coast. Rates are roughly half of peak Cala di Volpe.

faqs.question: Is there a beach at the hotel?
faqs.answer: Yes — Cala dei Ginepri, a small private cove reached by a path from the hotel, with sunbeds for guests. For classic long beaches, the concierge shuttles you to Spiaggia del Principe or Capriccioli.

faqs.question: Should we book Lucrezia in advance?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — it is a destination restaurant in high season. Reserve your first and last nights on arrival, and ask for a sea-view table.

faqs.question: Do we need a car?
faqs.answer: Yes, recommended. Shuttles handle the nearest beaches but a car gives you freedom to reach Porto Cervo, Phi Beach, and inland Gallura on your own schedule.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Early-to-mid June and first two weeks of September are the honeymoon sweet spots — warm sea, long evenings, quieter than August, and noticeably cheaper.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Hotel L'Ea Bianca Luxury Resort

Buongiorno,

We are planning our honeymoon for [DATES] and would love to stay 6 to 7 nights at L'Ea Bianca. Could you please send best-available rates for a Junior Suite Sea View with terrace, and for an Ea Bianca Suite with plunge pool?

Also:
1. Can you confirm your current adults-only minimum age?
2. Are any honeymoon packages available (champagne on arrival, private beach dinner, couple's spa treatment)?
3. Can you reserve a sea-view table at Lucrezia for our first and last evenings?
4. Can you arrange a private skippered boat for a day through La Maddalena?

Grazie mille,
[Your names]
